data.title: Hong Kong Establishes Virtual Asset Intelligence Task Force to Combat Cybercrime
data.description:
According to PANews, the Hong Kong Police Force's Cyber Security and Technology Crime Bureau hosted the Web3 Intelligence and Law Enforcement Summit today, where they officially launched the Virtual Asset Intelligence Task Force (VAIT).

The VAIT, led by the Hong Kong Police Force, comprises law enforcement agencies, financial regulators, and licensed virtual asset service providers. Its goal is to enhance the detection and prevention of crimes related to virtual assets and cybersecurity threats through intelligence sharing and collaboration.

Police Commissioner Chow Yat-ming reported that in the first eight months of this year, there were 1,463 cases related to virtual assets, including 15 hacking incidents targeting Web3 institutions, resulting in losses of approximately HKD 1.29 billion. The establishment of VAIT aims to promote compliance and security within the virtual asset ecosystem through a public-private partnership model, thereby strengthening market integrity and asset protection.

Previously, the Hong Kong Security Bureau had also formed a virtual asset intelligence task force to collaborate with relevant platforms in establishing a payment suspension mechanism.

data.title: OracleBNB Project on BNB Chain Experiences Rug Pull
data.description: According to Foresight News, PeckShield has reported that the OracleBNB project on the BNB Chain has undergone a rug pull. The project's team has deleted their social media accounts following the incident.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin sold three Meme coins for a profit of $96,000 and transferred $304,000 in ETH to a new wallet
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, Vitalik Buterin (vitalik.eth) sold 40.25 billion SPURDO, 10.31 billion MARVIN, and 6 trillion DOJO for 20.24 ETH, worth approximately $96,000. He then transferred 70 ETH (approximately $304,000) to a new wallet associated with the Mantle Foundation, which transferred the funds via RAILGUN.

data.title: Binance to List Walrus (WAL) on Multiple Platforms
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, Walrus (WAL) is set to be integrated into several Binance services, including Simple Earn, "Buy Crypto," Binance Convert, and Binance Margin. The WAL Flexible Products will be available on Binance Simple Earn starting 2025-10-10 07:30 (UTC), allowing users to subscribe to these products. Walrus (WAL) is part of the Binance HODLer Airdrops program, which rewards BNB holders with token airdrops based on historical snapshots of their BNB Simple Earn asset or BNB On-Chain Yield balances. Users can participate in BNB Simple Earn Products or BNB On-Chain Yields to automatically receive HODLer Airdrops rewards.

In the "Buy Crypto" section, users will have the option to purchase WAL using VISA, MasterCard, Google Pay, Apple Pay, or Revolut. Additionally, they can buy and sell WAL using their account balances on the "Buy Crypto" page, which will be accessible within one hour of WAL's listing on Binance Spot. For Binance Convert, users will be able to trade WAL against BTC, USDT, and other tokens at zero fees within one hour of its listing on Binance Spot. Furthermore, Binance Margin will introduce WAL as a new borrowable asset on both Cross and Isolated Margin, along with the WAL/USDT and WAL/USDC pairs, starting 2025-10-10 07:30 (UTC). Binance advises users to exercise caution and implement stringent risk management strategies when trading newly listed tokens due to their potential volatility. For the latest information on marginable assets, collateral ratios, and rates, users are encouraged to consult the Margin Data section.

data.title: Binance to Launch October Leaderboard Program with USDC Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce the October Monthly Leaderboard Program for Dual Investment users, offering rewards up to 5,888 USDC. The promotion will run from 2025-10-10 06:00 (UTC) to 2025-10-31 23:59 (UTC). Participants will be ranked based on their average Dual Investment subscription amount during this period. The top 100 eligible users will receive rewards in the form of a Dual Investment Subscription, with the highest reward being 5,888 USDC for the first place.

The reward structure is tiered, with the second place receiving 2,688 USDC, third place 1,088 USDC, and rewards decreasing incrementally down to 58 USDC for those ranked 51st to 100th. To qualify, users must maintain a subscription period of more than seven days. Dual Investment is described as a high-yield structured product allowing users to buy or sell cryptocurrency at a future date and price while earning rewards regardless of market direction. Supported tokens include BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, and others, with an annual percentage rate (APR) of 15% or more.

Participants must complete identity verification and join the promotion via the Activity page. Sub-accounts are not eligible, and once subscribed, users cannot cancel or redeem early. Rewards will be distributed within 14 days after the promotion ends. Binance reserves the right to disqualify participants acting fraudulently or not adhering to terms and conditions. The platform also retains the discretion to amend or cancel the promotion without prior notice. Additional terms and conditions apply, and discrepancies between English and translated versions should be referred to the original English content for accuracy.

data.title: Binance will launch WAL earning, buying, flash swap, and margin services
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance will launch Walrus (WAL) with flexible, easy-to-earn products and margin trading at 07:30 (UTC) on October 10, 2025, including WAL/USDT and WAL/USDC margin trading pairs. WAL will also be available for trading within one hour of the launch of Binance's buy and swap services. WAL will be available for purchase via various methods, including credit cards, and can be exchanged for BTC, USDT, and other tokens with zero fees. (This newsletter was generated by AI.)

data.title: Matrixport: Gold and Bitcoin rise in resonance, and the global financial landscape may usher in structural adjustments
data.description: Matrixport released a chart today, showing that in May 2023, gold fluctuated repeatedly around $2,000 per ounce, with central banks increasing their holdings for 11 consecutive months, providing momentum for a subsequent breakthrough. Gold's strong performance reflected a structural shift in the global monetary landscape, a trend not yet evident in the Bitcoin market at the time. Gold was still hovering around $2,000, while Bitcoin was trading around $30,000. This divergence, based on the historical correlation between the two in terms of momentum and valuation, suggests Bitcoin could potentially rally towards $45,000. Historically, gold typically experiences significant rallies only during crises, and this current surge confirms this pattern. Currently, the Federal Reserve's independence is under unprecedented pressure, while the United States and other major economies are implementing large-scale fiscal expansions. However, these policies have failed to generate a substantial growth multiplier effect. Instead, they have led to continued currency depreciation, structurally higher inflation, and long-term pressure on real economic growth.

data.title: A certain address spent $112,800 to build a position in Meme Rush and GIGGLE

Of this, $107,000 was spent on Meme Rush, costing $0.01377, and $6,314 on GIGGLE, costing $90.63. The address currently holds $1.41 million worth of BNB.

data.title: Crypto Market Shows Bearish Sentiment Despite Neutral Funding Rates
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass indicates that the cryptocurrency market remains neutral but is leaning towards bearish sentiment following a two-day decline. This trend is particularly evident for Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Solana (SOL).

Funding rates, a mechanism used by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain balance between contract prices and asset prices, are currently reflecting this sentiment. These rates are typically applied to perpetual contracts and facilitate the exchange of funds between long and short traders. The trading platforms do not collect these fees; instead, they adjust the cost or yield of holding contracts to keep contract prices close to asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% is considered the benchmark rate. When the rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, a rate below 0.005% suggests a bearish outlook.

data.title: ProsmLab strategically invests in AITR, an intelligent quantitative platform
data.description: ProsmLab has officially announced a strategic investment in AITR (AI Trading Reactor). This investment marks ProsmLab's deepening commitment to AI, DeFi, and Quantitative Analysis, aiming to advance the intelligentization of Web 3 finance. AITR, jointly developed by the Ton Foundation and FXCM, is a next-generation intelligent quantitative platform based on "AI big models + blockchain." AITR utilizes its proprietary deep learning framework, AIT-3, to integrate on-chain data and market sentiment in real time, enabling self-learning, millisecond-level trading decisions, and risk management capabilities. The project also aims to build an open financial ecosystem combining AI trading, token incentives, and user collaboration. Through a DAO governance mechanism, it allows investors, developers, and promoters to participate in the ecosystem's development.

data.title: Upbit to launch IN Korean Won trading pair
data.description: According to the official announcement, Upbit will launch the IN Korean won trading pair, and trading is expected to start at 14:30 on October 10 (Beijing time).

data.title: Influenced by the news of Uobit's listing, IN rose by more than 120% in a short period of time
data.description: Market data shows that IN briefly touched 0.3 USDT and is currently trading at 0.291 USDT, a 24-hour increase of 122%. Earlier news also surfaced that Upbit would be listing IN trading pairs against the Korean won.
